raw_text: irene nuez sanchez-gil irene.nunezsg @gmail.com +0034 682 77 42 08 spain professional experience 2015 currently altran on behalf of airbus d&s jul 2015 - currently safety reliability engineer (full time 40hrs/week) - project: new supersonic business jet. e todevelop the preliminary system safety assessment (pssa) for the fuel system (ata 28). e to develop the preliminary system safety assessment for the flight control system (ata 27). e to identify design enhancements regarding safety during the preliminary design phase for the fuel and flight control systems. - project: c-295 e to develop the preliminary system safety assessment for the hydraulic system (ata 29) and the electrical power (ata 24). e to develop the functional hazard assessment (fha) for the hydraulic system (ata 29) and the electrical power (ata 24). - project: a330 mrtt (multi role tanker transport) e to review and complete the wheel & tyre particular risk analysis (w&t pra) and ssas (multiples atas). e toreview supplier documentation for in-flight fuel dispensing system (ata 48) modifications. e tocontribute to the development of the master minimum equipment list (mmel). e todevelop the oxygen hazard analysis. tools: fault tree +, isograph reliability workbench internal training: fod (foreign object damage) course (6h) advanced avionic course (12h) basic aircraft maintenance course (12h) 2013 - 2015 airbus group dec 2014 - jul 2015 airworthiness and certification engineer (full time 40hrs/week) - responsible for all airbus fleet for the airborne auxiliary power and inert gas system. - to carry out changes to type certificate and airworthiness or operational compliance demonstration for any program (new, in development and used aircraft). - to identify and interpret the applicable certification basis (easa, jaa, faa) and negotiate the acceptable means of compliance. - responsible for in-service occurrence assessment process. - to provide support on any airworthiness matters to the designers and to other airbus functions. tools: sdm, icc, aircam, mpt, mct, nacre, acc2, taksy. nov 2013 nov 2014 safety 8 reliability engineer trainee (full time 40hrs/week) - management and review of technical safety and reliability documentation at aircraft level (safety plan, cdd, pra/zsa/hea, etc.), system level (fha/pssa/ssa, cma, t105g, t205g) and equipment level (fmea/fmes, fta, iha/eha). - reliability technical analysis accomplished and presented to airbus engineering community. - aircraft system knowledge for the in-service airbus fleet and new developments in electrical power, bleed, inert gas system and airborne auxiliary power. - support to electrical generation area, under responsibility of spain design office. 2012 - 2013 nucleo duro felguera internship in marketing 8 sales engineering department (part time 40hrs/week) - internal data base creation and management of company projects (tools: ms excel, ms access, mysql) - communication with suppliers and subsequent knowledge of their equipment (rockwell automation, emerson, supermicro, tradesegur, schneider, etc.) - management and development of equipment sales for customers with non-interrupted electrical network service contracted. - knowledge in non-interrupted power units, scadas, plcs, electrical installations, etc. - support to sales engineering department, working with the team in charge of the technical documentation developing projects for airport and electrical sectors. - internal courses: electrical protection course, scada sherpa/dms course, foreign trade course. education 2008 - 2013 polytechnique university of madrid (spain) degree in aeronautical engineering - main subjects: air navigation systems, avionics, electronic, electrical installations, air transport. - degree final project: control and management system of the new electrical installation at santiago de compostela airport. complementary education 2015 (12h) senasa easa part m - continuing airworthiness requirements -
